def: s.f. = phaṅkī; phāṅkī, qq.v.
s.f. A preconceived opinion, previous statement, &c. = phāṅkī, q.v.
s.f. = phakkikā; phāṅkī, qq.v.
(see phāṅknā), To be chucked into the mouth from the palm of the hand (grain, meal, powder, &c.).
s.m. An indecent talker; an absurd prater; a fop ( = phāṅkaṛ).
s.f. Absurdity; raillery, scurrility; — foppishness.
s.m. = phāg, q.v.
s.f. The act of treating with rudeness; rudeness, dishonour, disgrace
subs: (-kī), To treat with rudeness, to vilify, disgrace; to speak ill (of), disparage, slander.

def: s.m. A ploughshare; — an epithet of Mahā-deva, and of Bala-rāma.
s.m. A lump or piece of betel-nut, Areca; — a step, stride, leap ( = phalāng).

s.m. Fruit; produce, product, crop, yield; offspring, children; return, requital, recompense; gain, profit, advantage; result, effect, consequence; inference, corollary; (in Arith.) the quotient; the answer (of a problem, riddle, &c.); the area or superficial contents (of a figure); the fulfilment (of an omen); — the iron head (of a spear, arrow, &c.); the blade (of a sword, knife, &c.); a ploughshare; a shield; a kind of fragrant berry and drug (commonly called kakoli); a nutmeg
subs: (phala+apekshā), s.f. Regard to results, expectation of consequences
(phala+āśī), adj. & s.m. Feeding or living on fruits; — any creature that lives on fruits
v.n. Fruit to appear; to bear fruit
s.m. Taking a slight repast, eating fruits, parched corn, &c. for a meal; — a repast of fruits, &c.
phal-bujhauwal, s.m. A boys' game (called also mankelā: 'think of a number; double it; add ten to it; take five from it, &c.: how much remains? Twenty-one:' the number was eight)
s.m. Enjoyment of consequences; possession of rent or profit, usufruct
adj. & s.m. Enjoying fruits or consequences; receiving profits; — one who reaps consequences, profits, &c.

s.m. The ripening of fruit; the fulness of consequences; — the tree and fruit Carissa carandas (com. called karaundā); — phal-pākāntā (i.e. pāka+antā), s.f. An annual plant which dies after bearing fruit
(-kā), To reap the fruits or consequences (of), to reap the reward (of), to get one's deserts
part. adj. Obtaining the consequences (of actions), rewarded, recompensed; punished
s.f. Obtaining (the desired) fruit or result; enjoying the consequences (of actions)
phal-phalālī, phal-phalyārī, phal-phalairī, s.f. Fruits of various kinds, fruitage
s.m. lit. 'Full of kernels'; the common citron
s.m. The fruit-bearing tāṛ, the female palm
phala-traya, s.m. 'Fruit-triad'; three sorts of fruit collectively (the fruit of the vine with those of Grewia asiatica or Xylocarpus granatum and Gmelina arborea); the three myrobalans
s.m. The bark of the Indian fig-tree (ground and eaten with curds by way of penance)

phalad, phal-dātā, phal-dātr̤i, phal-dāʼī, phal-dāyak, adj. & s.m. Yielding or bearing fruit; bringing profit or gain; yielding a result or consequence; giving a reward; fertile; profitable, advantageous, useful, efficacious; — one who rewards; a benefactor; — phala-da, phalad, s.m. A fruit-tree; a tree
s.f. Acquiring fruit, reaping fruit, realizing an object; a prosperous issue
phal-sampad, s.f. Abundance of fruit; good result; success, prosperity
s.f. Desire of a result or consequence
s.m. Produce of trees and orchards; profits or revenue arising from the fruit-trees on an estate
s.m. The fruit of the Carissa carandas (syn. karondā)
s.m. The destruction of fruits; frustration of results; disappointing
s.m. 'Having fruit for hair'; the cocoa-nut tree
adj. Fruitful, bearing fruit in due season
phal-vat, phal-vant, adj. Bearing fruit, fruitful; yielding results or consequences
(phala+uttamā), s.f. 'Best of fruits,' a kind of grape without stones; the highest recompense, viz. that arising from sacred study
(phala+udaya), s.m. Coming forth or appearance of fruit; profit, advantage, gain; appearance of consequences or results; consequence, result, recompense, reward; punishment
s.f. Loss of fruit or profit
adj. Void of fruit, yielding no fruit, fruitless

def: s.m. A ploughshare ( = phāl).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī) Abounding with fruit; — having a blade, or head (as a knife, &c.; e.g. du-phalā, 'two-bladed'); — a compound letter; the head (of a dart, or arrow), the head or bowl (of a spoon).
adj. & s.m. See s.v. phal for compounds of which that word is the first member.
s.f. A kind of fish ( = phallakī, q.v.).
s.f. = phalyārī, q.v.
s.m. A board; a bench; a layer, base; surface; a leaf for writing on; a shield; the bone of the forehead, Os frontis; — the plant Mesua ferrea.
s.m. One who is armed with a shield, a shield-bearer.
s.m. Flannel.
s.m. Bearing fruit, fructifying; producing consequences.
v.t. To cause to bear fruit, to make fruitful, to fructify.
s.f. A long stride; a leap, jump, spring, bound
subs: v.n. To stride; to spring, leap, jump ( = phalāṅgnā).

def: s.f. = phalāṅg, q.v.
v.n. To stride; to spring, leap, jump; — v.t. To stride over; to spring or leap over.
v.n. = phalāṅgnā, q.v.
s.m. = phalās, q.v.
adj. Bearing fruit, fruitful.
s.m. = H پهلاري फलारी phalārī, s.f. = phalyār; phalyārī, qq.v.
s.f. = H پهلار फलार phalār, s.m. = phalyār; phalyārī, qq.v.
s.m. A stride; a leap, spring, jump ( = phalāṅg).
s.m. The bread-fruit or jak-tree ( = panas).
s.f. The state of being fruitful or luxuriant, fruitfulness.
s.f. See s.v. phal.
s.f. The opposite-leaved fig-tree, Ficus oppositifolia; the red powder used in the Holī festival ( = phāg, q.v.); — name of a particular river said to flow under Gayā.
s.m. The Hindū month phāgun (Feb.-March); a name of Arjun; — the tree Pentaptera arjuna.
s.m. = phālgun, q.v.

s.f. The day of full moon in the month phālgun (on which the Holī or great vernal festival of the Hindūs is celebrated); — the sixteenth and seventeenth lunar asterisms (distinguished as pūrva and uttara, 'the former' and 'the latter').
s.f. A ploughshare ( = phāl).
s.f. A cod or pod (esp. of peas); the seed-vessel or shell of any leguminous plant (as peas, mūṅg, lobiyā, uṛad, &c.); — a shield; — a loop
subs: s.m. A hook for drawing the looped strings through the holes by which the walls of a tent are laced to the top.

def: adj. Bearing fruit, fruitful, &c. ( = phalwān); having an iron head or point (as an arrow, &c.); having a blade, bladed (as a knife; e.g. du-phalī, two-bladed).
s.f. = phalinī, q.v.
s.f. (of phalī, q.v.), A medicinal plant ( = priyaṅgu, q.v.); — a species of pot-herb, Echites dichotoma; — a species of flower, Celosia cristata.
s.m. A meaning implied or involved (though not expressed); — adv. On the whole, in effect, in fine.
s.f. A pod, &c. = phalī, q.v.
v.n. To bear fruit.
s.m. A species of fresh-water fish, Mystus capirat (syn. phalsaʼī, phalaʼī).

ety: phāl˚ = Prk. फाले(इ), or फाल(इ); S. स्फालय(ति), rt. स्फल्
def: v.n. To spring, leap, jump (com. phāṅdnā).

ety: phal˚ = Prk. फल(इ) = S. फल(ति), rt. फल्;+nā or anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To bear fruit, be in fruit, to produce; to have issue; to result, be produced as a consequence; to be fortunate, to prosper; — to break out into blisters, pimples, &c., to have an eruption on the face and body
subs: v.n. To bud and blossom; to flourisb, thrive.

def: s.m. See s.v. phal.

ety: S. & Prk. फल+Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. The blade (of a sword, knife, &c.).
s.m. The fruit Grewia asiatica (com. written fālsā).
s.m. Door, gate; quarter (of a town).
s.f. A species of fresh-water fish, Mystus capirat (syn. phallakī; phalaʼī).
s.m. See s.v. phal.
s.m. A small shoot.
adj. & adv. Spare, surplus; — to spare, over and above ( = fāltū, q.v.).
s.m. A knotted fringe or border; a knotted tassel.
adj. See s.v. phal.
s.m. A bud; a flower.
A light meal or repast of fruits, vegetables, or parched grain (see phalāhār, s.v. phal).
A light meal or repast of fruits, vegetables, or parched grain (see phalāhār, s.v. phal).
s.m. The expanded hood or neck of a snake (esp. of the Cobra di capello)
subs: v.n. To expand its hood (a snake)
phaṇ-kar, s.m. A snake (esp. the Cobra)
s.m. A jewel supposed to be found in the hood of a snake
(-par), To strike with the head, to bite or sting (a snake); — to strike the head (against); to make strenuous efforts
phaṇ-want, adj. & s.m. Hooded; — a snake ( = phaṇ-dhar).

def: s.f. A snake's hood ( = phan); — the forecastle of a ship.
s.m. A shoemaker's wedge, a wedge.
s.m. = panas, q.v.
s.m. Noose, snare, gin, net; a long stick with a noose attached; (met.) perplexity, difficulty
s.m. Leap, spring, jump.
s.f. = phāṅdī, q.v.
s.m. Noose, net, snare, trap, gin; grasp, gripe, toils, clutches; perplexity, difficulty; maze
subs: (-meṅ), To get entangled, to get into knots (a string, &c.); to be noosed; to be strangled
phandā ḍālnā, phandā lagānā, v.n. To set a snare, trap, &c.; to knot, loop, entangle, &c.

phande-meṅ paṛnā, phande-meṅ phaṅsnā, v.n. To fall into a snare, trap, &c., to be caught in a trap, &c., to be ensnared; to be taken in, be imposed upon; to fall into difficulties; to fall into the toils (of, -ke).

def: s.m. = phāṅd, 'Noose.'
s.m. Noose, net, snare, trap, gin; grasp, gripe, toils, clutches; perplexity, difficulty; maze
subs: (-meṅ), To get entangled, to get into knots (a string, &c.); to be noosed; to be strangled
phandā ḍālnā, phandā lagānā, v.n. To set a snare, trap, &c.; to knot, loop, entangle, &c.

phande-meṅ paṛnā, phande-meṅ phaṅsnā, v.n. To fall into a snare, trap, &c., to be caught in a trap, &c., to be ensnared; to be taken in, be imposed upon; to fall into difficulties; to fall into the toils (of, -ke).

def: v.t. To cause to jump or leap; to cause to leap or jump over.
s.f. A bundle or load of sugar-cane (generally from fifty to a hundred).
v.t. To take in a noose, snare, or trap; to entrap, ensnare; to entangle; to inveigle; to beguile, divert ( = phuslānā).
v.t. To catch with a noose, to noose, ensnare; to tie, imprison; to entangle, entrap.

ety: phāṅd˚ = Prk. फंदे(इ), or फंदे(इ); S. स्पन्दय(ति), rt. स्पन्द्
def: v.n. To leap, jump, spring; — v.t. To spring or jump over.
v.n. To be taken in a noose, snare, or trap; to be ensnared, be entrapped; to be imprisoned; to be entangled; to be caught or held fast; to get into difficulties.
s.m. A grasshopper.
s.f. Flour or meal mixed with curds.
adj. & s.m. Hooded, &c. = phaṇin, q.v.; — a wedge (so named from its shape, = phānā, q.v.).
s.m. Marjoram, and another similar plant.
s.f. A plant (apparently) a species of basil with small leaves.
s.m. A snake, serpent.
s.m. See s.v. phaṇin.
s.m. A quail.
s.m. The hooded serpent, a cobra di capello; a snake
subs: s.m. An epithet of Śeshnāg; a name of Patanjali

def: s.m. See s.v. phaṇin.
s.m. See s.v. phaṇin.
s.f. The plant Clerodendrum siphonanthus
s.f. The plant Clerodendrum siphonanthus; the plant Lipeocercis serrata; — the plant Alhagi maurorum.
s.m. As much of anything as can be thrown or taken into the mouth; bit, piece, slice (of fruit), flake; a clove (of garlic); — cut, opening, aperture (in fruit, &c.)
subs: v.t. To chuck (grain, &c.), into the mouth ( = phāṅknā).

def: s.m. = phāṅk, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Smart, dashing, fine, flashy; spirited, daring, bold, impudent; robust, burly, lusty; — a smart fellow, &c.; a fop.
s.f. An objection (in logic); a proposition or first part of an argument; a sophism; illusion, trick, fraud.
s.f. A mouthful of dry grain, powder, &c.; a piece or slice of fruit.
s.f. = phaṅkā; phāṅkī, q.v.
s.f. = phaṅkā; phāṅkī, q.v.

v.t. To chuck (dry grain, meal, powder, &c. or anything dry) into the mouth from the palm of the hand; to slice, slit; — v.n. To open out, to part asunder, to gape; to disappear; — to talk without ceasing.
adj. & s.m. Smart, dashing, fine, flashy; spirited, daring, bold, impudent; robust, burly, lusty; — a smart fellow, &c.; a fop.
s.m. corr. of fānūs, q.v.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Swollen, &c.
s.f. The hiss of a snake.

v.n. To expand the hood (as a snake); to hiss (as a snake); to spring up quickly or suddenly (as a snake, or as a fast-growing plant); to be or become erect, to rise; to move about briskly (as a playful child), to frisk.
s.m. A hole, orifice.
v.n. To swell, rise, be swollen; to be puffed up, be bloated.
s.m. A splinter (of bamboo, &c.); anything such as grease, dirt, &c. which gets under the nails and occasions pain.

ety: S. पाश, पाशक; but cf. phāṅs above
def: s.m. A noose, &c. ( = phāṅd); an impediment; — the share of the bākhar plough; — a die for playing with.

ety: S. पाश, पाशक; but cf. phāṅs above
def: s.m. A noose, &c. ( = phāṅd); an impediment; — the share of the bākhar plough; — a die for playing with.

ety: caus. of phaṅsnā; — phaṅsā˚ = phaṇsāw˚ = Prk. फंसावे(इ) or फंसाव(इ) = S. स्पृश+आपि
def: v.t. To cause to be snared or noosed; to cause to be caught or entrapped; to cause to be involved; to ensnare, entrap, atch; to inveigle, entangle, involve (in), to implicate.
s.m. Entanglement; an ensnaring or entrapping business or affair; a sticking place (as a muddy road, a bog, or fen); a narrow place.
s.f. Noose, loop, snare; halter; strangulation, hanging, death by hanging: phāṅsī pānā, phāṅsī paṛnā, v.n. To be hanged
subs: v.n. To have the noose put on the neck; to mount the scaffold, to be hanged
phāṅsī-kā denā, v.t. To strangle, to hang
phāṅsī-gar, s.m. A strangler; a robber and murderer who strangles his victims by means of a turban or cloth, a ṭhag.

def: s.m. A foot-pad who strangles travellers, a ṭhag ( = phāṅsī-gar, q.v.); a hangman.
v.n. = phaṅsnā, q.v.; — v.t. = phaṅsānā, q.v.

ety: phaṅs˚ = Prk. फंस(इ) = S. स्पृश(ति), rt. स्पृस्
def: v.n. To be taken in a noose, &c.; to be noosed, be ensnared, be entrapped; to be suspended (as a vessel with a noose round it); to be caught; to be imprisoned; to be entangled; to stick (as in mud, or in a narrow passage); to be impeded; to be embroiled, be involved (in difficulties, calamity, &c.).
s.f. = phāṅsī, q.v.
v.t. = phaṅsānā, q.v.
s.m. A village register.
s.m. = fānūs, q.v.
s.m. & adv. A small quantity given above the quantity purchased; — over and above, to boot (cf. phāltū; — syn. ghaluʼā, rūkhan, set-kā).
s.m. A mattock, spade.

s.f. A shovel, hoe, an instrument like a small rake or hoe for removing a horse's dung; — a crutch on which a jogī leans; — a piece of wood or staff with a support at each end (which is held in both hands and laid horizontally in performing the exercise of ḍanḍ; — syn. bairāgā).
v.n. = phadphadānā, q.v.
s.m. A blister, vesicle; a bubble
subs: v.n. To be blistered; (met.) to be afflicted in mind
v.n. To satisfy a revenge that has long been rankling in one's breast; to give vent to one's passion, appease one's wrath.

def: adj. Swelled, swollen, puffed, inflated, distended, bloated, fat, corpulent, flabby; — insipid, weak, watery (also written phophsā).
s.f. = phaphūṅdī, q.v.
s.f. Mouldiness; mould (on fruit, bread, &c. from damp), mildew
subs: v.n. To be mildewed, be mouldy.

def: s.m. A blister ( = phapholā).
s.m. = phapholā, q.v.
adj. = phapphas and phaphsā, q.v.
adj. Swelled, swollen, puffed, inflated, distended, bloated, fat, corpulent, flabby; — insipid, weak, watery (also written phophsā).
s.m. Pretence; hypocritical show.
s.m. A layer of mud on a wooden roof.
adj. = phapphas and phaphsā, q.v.
s.f. = phaphūṅdī, q.v.
v.n. To be mildewed ( = phaphūṅdī lagnā, q.v.s.v. phaphūṅdī).
s.m. (Prov.) = phal, q.v.

s.f. A place of public business or of public resort; a place where goods in quantity are exposed for inspection or sale; a mart, exchange, &c.; a gambling-house (esp. where dice are played); — the shafts or pole of a carriage; a gun-carriage; the stand of a great gun
subs: s.m. A gamester, gambler; swaggerer, prater, talkative fellow
s.f. Gambling, playing at dice; — phaṛ-pe rakhnā, phaṛ-pe lagānā, v.t. To wager, to stake.

def: s.m. Rent, split, crack, fissure.
adj. (f. -ī), Half-dried, half-dry; dry.
A flag, standard, vane, pennant.
A flag, standard, vane, pennant.
adj. = pharahrā, q.v.
s.m. = H پهريري फरैरी pharairī, s.f. = pharahrā; pharahrī, q.v.
s.f. = H پهريرا फरैरा pharairā, s.m. = pharahrā; pharahrī, q.v.
s.m. A dice-player, a gambler ( = phaṛ-bāz).
s.m. A shield ( = phar).
s.f. = phaṛak, q.v.
s.f. Fluttering, flutter, flap; fluctuating; vibrating, quivering, twitching, pulsation, throbbing, palpitating, palpitation; agitation (cf. bharak); weltering (in blood)
subs: adv. After repeated fluttering or fluctuation (between hope and despair).

def: v.n. = phaṛaknā, q.v.

v.n. To flutter, to twitch convulsively, vibrate with convulsive involuntary motion (as the eyelids, &c.), to quiver, throb, beat, pulsate, palpitate; to writhe (cf. bhaṛaknā); to welter (in blood, as a wounded person) — to yearn or long (for).
v.t. To cause to be torn, split, or cleft, &c. (cf. phaṭānā).
s.m. = pharrās, q.v.
s.f. Wrangling, squabbling; squabble, quarrel, fight.
s.m. = pharćhā, q.v.
v.t. = pharćhānā, q.v.

s.m. Clearance (as of the sky, &c.); dispersion (of clouds, or of a multitude of people); fair weather; settlement; liquidation; adjustment; decision, definitive sentence (of a judge); acquittal, release, discharge; — adj. ( = S. parishkṛita), Clear, fair, serene (as the sky); clean, polished, bright; pure; fair, honest, candid
s.f. Cleanness, brightness; clearness (of the sky, &c.); settled state of the weather; pureness, honesty, fairness.
v.t. To clean, polish; to wipe; to clear; clear away (as the clouds, &c.); to settle, adjust, wipe off (debts, &c.); to decide, give a final sentence ( = faiṣala karnā).
s.f. A species of carp; — or (S. phali), a kind of fish com. called phalai.
s.f. A shield, buckler (esp. a small leather shield used in fencing)
s.f. A slab, block (of stone), a large stone (the poet Saudā says: Agar Saudā ko ćherā hai, to laṛko mol-lo phaṛiyāṅ; tasallī mujh dīwāne-ko na ho jholī-ke pattharoṅ-se).
s.f. Cricket; grasshopper; locust ( = patangā).
s.m. A pharisee (used in the translation of the New Testament).
s.m. A pharisee (used in the translation of the New Testament).
s.f. A short bordered skirt worn by girls.
s.m. One who takes a contract for reaping.
s.m. The keeper of a gaming house or dice table; — a retailer of goods; pedlar, huckster.
s.f. = phaṛak, q.v.
v.t. To cause to flutter; to put in a flutter to cause convulsive motion in the muscles; to cause to throb, &c.; — (met.) to shew; to show off, to make a display.
s.f. = phaṛkan; phaṛak q.v.
s.m. Pomp, show, display ( = dikhāʼo).
s.f. A coarse screen, a partition.

ety: phaṛ˚ = Prk. फाडे(इ), or फाड(इ); S. स्फाटय(ति), rt. स्फट्
def: v.t. To tear, rend, split, sever, break, cleave; to tear open, burst, rip, lacerate, mangle; — to clarify by melting or boiling; to coagulate
subs: v.t. To tear to pieces, to mangle, lacerate
v.t. To tear, rend (as a wild beast), to worry, bite, gnaw; — phāṛ-khāʼū, adj. & s.m. Ravenous; — a ravenous beast

def: s.f. A mixture of parched rice and barley used as food (see ćabenā).

ety: S. परि, or प्रति+स्पन्द; or प्रपञ्च
def: s.m. Deceit, trick, ruse, strategem, wile; cheat, rascality, wickedness.
adj. & s.m. Artful, tricky, deceitful, treacherous, wicked; — an artful person, trickster, cheat, knave, &c.
adj. & s.m. Artful, tricky, deceitful, treacherous, wicked; — an artful person, trickster, cheat, knave, &c.
s.f. Flapping (of wings); fluttering (of a flag); crackling (of stiff paper); pattering (of rain, &c.); rattling (of musketry), &c.; — adv. With a flapping sound, &c.
subs: v.n. To flap, flutter, &c. = phaṛphaṛānā, q.v.

def: s.f. Flutter; struggle; disquietude, agitation.

v.n. To shake, shiver, to move with convulsive motion, to struggle, to be agitated; to flap (the wings), to flutter, to wave, to twinkle; to crackle (as stiff paper, &c.); to patter (as rain); to rattle (as musketry).
s.f. = phaṛphaṛāhat, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Active, brisk, swift, quick, hasty, bustling: — a flutterer.
s.m. A root (as ginger, &c) which separates into cloves, a race or sprig; an end or a fragment of a branch; the stalk of maize, &c.

ety: S. स्फर् = स्फुर्+H. caus. aff. ānā
def: v.n. To flutter, flap, wave (as a flag, a bird, &c.); to whiz, whir, whistle; to snort (as a horse).
s.m. A species of fir (so called from the whistling or whizzing of its branches in the wind).

r: pharrāṭā, pharrā, pharrā-nā, āṭā, āṭ, ātu
ety: pharrā, rt. of pharrā-nā+āṭā = āṭ = S. aff. ātu
def: s.m. Flapping (of a flag, &c.); fluttering (of a bird); snorting, snort (of a horse); tramping, tramp (of a swiftly-running animal); — the staff of a flag; a piece of bamboo
subs: v.n. To take flight, to fly or flee away.

def: s.m. A hatchet, an axe.
s.m. = phāʼorā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of wooden vessel.
s.m. = pharwā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of hoe or rake (see phāʼoṛā).
s.m. A betel-box.
v.t. = pharānā, q.v.
adj. Lagging behind; last (in order, degree, or time); — adv. Behind; late.
v.n. To become loose or slack; — to sink (into mud, &c.); — to split, burst open, break.
s.m. Worthless or bad tobacco. (Inelegant.)
v.t. To cause to stick (as in mud, &c.); to cause to be imprisoned; to involve (one in difficulties, &c.); to entangle, ensnare, embroil, catch; to squash; — to take in, deceive, cheat

ety: phasā˚, rt. of phasānā+Prk. एअव्वअं+ट्ट = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: v.n. To get into difficulties, &c.
v.t. = phasānā, q.v.

ety: phasā˚, rt. of phasānā+Prk. एअव्वअं+ट्ट = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: v.n. To get into difficulties, &c.

ety: phasā˚, rt. of phasānā+Prk. एअव्वअं+ट्ट = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: v.n. To get into difficulties, &c.
s.m. A strangler, a ṭhag ( = phāṅsī-gar, q.v.).
v.t. To split, cleave, break, burst open; to loosen, slacken.
s.m. A sitting broadly and roomily or loosely upon the buttocks and hams; sitting on the ground with the legs extended; squatting
subs: phaskaṛā mār-ke baiṭhnā, v.n. To sit in the posture described above, to squat.

def: s.m. A sitting broadly and roomily or loosely upon the buttocks and hams; sitting on the ground with the legs extended; squatting
subs: phaskaṛā mār-ke baiṭhnā, v.n. To sit in the posture described above, to squat.

def: s.m. A sitting broadly and roomily or loosely upon the buttocks and hams; sitting on the ground with the legs extended; squatting
subs: phaskaṛā mār-ke baiṭhnā, v.n. To sit in the posture described above, to squat.

def: s.f. A handful of grain, &c. taken by official personages as a tax out of each load brought to market; a small quantity (of any commodity) given over the weight or measure ( = phāʼo); a palmful of grain presented (on occasions) by schoolboys to their master; a handful of grain presented by women to Śiva on every Monday throughout the year, or throughout the month Sāvan.
v.n. = phaṅsnā, q.v.

ety: S. पाश+dim. aff. ṛī fr. S. रः
def: s.f. = phāṅsī, q.v.
s.m. Division; division of revenue, assessment among the sharers in joint tenantry; — breadth.
s.f. = phaṭ; (also) the creaking sound of a shoe, &c.
subs: s.m. Sound of a thump, or of the cracking of a whip, &c., or of the beating of wet clothes against a stone; — a long whip used in breaking-in horses

v.t. To crack (a whip), to whip, lash; to beat clothes on a stone (in washing); to rail at, &c. ( = phiṭ-kārnā); — to snap up, make off with; to get hold of, to make, to win; — to get rid of, part with, dispose of.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Torn, split, rent, broken, cracked; — s.m. Crack, rent, opening, gap, breach, fissure
subs: s.m. Turned or sour milk; whey
To stand in the gap or breach; to put one's finger in the pie (of), to interfere or meddle with; to burn one's finger in another's quarrel.

def: s.m. A gate, door, entrance; a large shutter; a watchman in charge of a gate; — a bar, obstruction, impediment, check; the bar of a court of justice where the plaintiff and defendant take their station; — a pound
subs: v.t. To put in the pound, to impound.

ety: phaṭak˚ = Prk. फट्टक्क(इ), or फट्टक्क(इ); S. स्फट+कृ
def: v.t. To winnow; to sift, to strain; to dust, to shake or knock off any light thing which slightly adheres (as dust from a table, or crumbs from a table-cloth); — v.n. To interfere, intermeddle ( = daḵẖal denā, usually with negative adverb); to be separated; to keep or hold aloof (from), to fight shy (of); — to pay a flying visit.
s.m. The tape in a pellet bow which strikes the ball.
v.t. To split, &c. = phāṛnā, q.v.
s.m. Splitting, &c.; gap, separation, interval, break.
s.m. Crystal.
s.m. = patingā, patangā, q.v.
s.f. Chaff, or husk (which flies off in winnowing); particles which remain after sifting or straining (flour, &c.), siftings.
s.m. See s.v. phaṭ and phiṭ.
s.f. Sulphate of alumina, alum.
v.t. See s.v. phaṭ and phiṭ.
s.f. A fowler's net; a hunter's bag; a large cage; a clapper (tied to a tree to frighten birds with).
v.n. = phaṭnā, q.v.; — and v.t. = phaṭānā, q.v.

ety: phaṭ˚ = Prk. फट्ट(इ); S. स्फद्य(ते); rt. स्फट्
def: v.n. To be torn, be split, be rent, be cracked, be broken; to tear, split, crack, burst, break; to be separated, be detached; to be dispersed (as clouds); to be clarified (as milk, oil, &c., the impure part being separated from the pure); to turn, become sour (as milk); to break out (as sores); — to tremble (with fear), break into a tremor
subs: v.n. (intensive) To burst open; to be produced plentifully or copiously; to be bursting (as it were) with fat, &c.; to become fat suddenly; — to be overworked, to be confounded with too much business
v.n. intensive of, and = phaṭnā, q.v.

def: v.t. To beat, shake, or flap (the wings, as birds about to fly); — v.n. To give forth a dull or creaking sound (as the shoe of a person walking).
s.m. (local) = patthar, q.v.
s.m. = phāʼoṛā, q.v.
v.n. = phabnā, q.v.
s.m. A flock of cotton (used for scenting, or as lint to dress a wound); a plaster, pledget (syn. phūhā or phoyā, q.v.).
v.t. See pheṅknā (the proper form).
s.f. Remnants of food, leavings of a meal, droppings from the mouth; refuse, orts.
s.f. = phel, q.v.
s.m. Froth, foam, scum:- phen-vān, phen-want, adj. Frothing, foaming; frothy, foamy.
s.m. Froth, foam, scum:- phen-vān, phen-want, adj. Frothing, foaming; frothy, foamy.
s.m. Cuttle-fish bone.
s.f. The soap-berry.
adj. Frothy, &c. = phenil, q.v.
v.n. To foam, froth ( = phinānā, q.v.).
s.f. Frothiness, foaminess; vapour.
adj. Foamy, &c. ( = phenil, q.v.).
adj. & s.f. Frothy, &c. = phenil, q.v.; — a kind of sweetmeat.
adj. & s.m. Frothy, foamy, spumous; — the soap-plant, Sapindus detergens; — the plant Zizyphus jujuba, and its fruit; — the fruit of the Vangueria spinosa.
s.f. Throw, cast, fling; a handful of money, &c. scattered or thrown to be scrambled for (syn. bakher; nićhāwar).

ety: rt. of pheṅk-nā+āʼit = Prk. आइत्तो or आइंतो or आअंतो = आवंतो or आवेंतो = S. आपयन्, the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. increment आपि
def: s.m. Thrower, flinger, &c.; a cudgel-player, a fencer.

ety: rt. of pheṅknā+āʼu or aʼo = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: adj. Requiring to be thrown; fit to be thrown away.

ety: pheṅk˚ = Prk. पेकव(इ) or पेंख(इ) = S. प्रेक्ष्य(ति), future of प्र+इष्
def: v.t. To throw, fling, cast, hurl, pitch; to dart; to let fly (a hawk, &c. at game); to set (a horse) off at full speed, to gallop; to ride full tilt (at, -par); to shed, spill, pour out; to brandish, flourish (a stick or cudgel); to throw away, squander, &c. (in these senses the more common form is pheṅk-denā)
subs: v.t. (intensive of pheṅknā), To throw away, fling away; to waste, squander, lavish; to slight, disregard, make light of; to fly (a hawk after, -par, game); to ride (a horse) full tilt (at, -par).

def: v.t. = phiṅkwānā, phikwānā, q.v.
s.m. Mucus of the nose ( = poṅṭā).
s.f. Waist-band, belt, fob; the waist (when belted)
subs: (-par), To gird up the loins (for), to get ready (for), to resolve (on).

def: s.m. A waist-band (without a fringe); a small turban.

ety: dim. of pheṅṭ or pheṅṭā, q.v.
def: s.f. A skein (of thread, &c.; = aṭṭī; āṅṭī, q.v.).
v.t. To mix by trituration; to beat up or whip (eggs, &c.); to beat up into froth.
s.m. The milk of a cow, &c. for some days after calving, biestings.
s.f. Inability to walk, move, or stir (cf. phepṛī).
s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: S. फुस्फुस+Prk. डो; S. रः or लः
def: s.m. The lungs, lights.
s.m. = phephṛā, q.v
s.f. Dryness or parchedness of the lips; scales on the lips.
adv. Again; back, &c. = phir, q.v.

s.m. Turning, twisting; turn, twist, coil, fold, bend, curvature, winding, meander, maze; crookedness, obliquity; return; circumference, round, circuitousness, distance round; equivocation, ambiguity; change; variation; difference; discrepancy, deficiency; loss, damage; alternation, revolution, vicissitude; reverse, misfortune; difficulty, perplexity, straits, dilemma
subs: v.n. To be round about or circuitous; — a difference or discrepancy to arise, to differ, to be discrepant or deficient
s.m. (intensive and frequentative of pher), Constant or repeated change; frequent turns or windings, &c.; alternations, vicissitudes; — adj. Alternate; — pher-phār karnā, v.n. To alternate, &c.
v.n. To wind (as a river, road, &c.), to meander; to go a roundabout way, to make a circuit; to experience a reverse of fortune, to meet with difficulties
or pher-meṅ paṛnā (-ke), To fall into the whirl or rotation (of); to fall into straits or difficulties, be involved in misfortune
v.t. To involve (one) in difficulties, place one in a dilemma or straits; to throw obstacles in the way (of another); to mislead.

ety: phera = pher, q.v.+Prk. अओ = S. कः
def: s.m. Turning; round, circuit; perambulation; walking (of the bride and bridegroom) round (the sacred fire); returning, return; alternating, alternation; — a roll; — a wooden frame with which lime, sand, &c. is measured (so called from its being passed over the lime, &c.)
subs: pherā-pherī, s.f. Frequent change, &c. ( = pher-phār); change, exchange, interchange; returning, alternating, alternation; walking backwards and forwards; going and coming repeatedly, visiting, visits
(-ke), To lead (a bride) round (the sacred fire), to marry, give in marriage
(-ke), To be led round (the sacred fire), to be married.

def: v.t. = phirānā (the correct form).
adj. & s.m. Fraudulent, cratty; malicious, noxious, injurious; — a rogue, a cheat; a jackal; a rākshas, a demon.

s.f. Going round; circumambulation (round an object = pradakshiṇā; parikramā); circuit, round; hawking; the rounds of a pedlar, or of a wandering mendicant; the profession of a wandering beggar, mendicancy; — slice or cut round, joint of a sugar-cane, &c.); — shift, artifice, trick, subtlety, finesse
subs: v.n. To go round hawking, to hawk; to go round begging
s.m. A hawker, pedlar ( = bisātī; phaṛīyā).

ety: pher˚ = Prk. फेर(इ) or फेरे(इ) = S. पर्ये(ति), rt. इ with परि
def: v.t. To turn, twist, bend; to turn round, whirl, twirl; to turn back, turn away, reject, send back; to return, give back, restore, refund; to turn one (from, or against, -kī t̤araf-se); to bring or draw one (over to one's side, -kī t̤araf); to turn over, invert, reverse, turn inside out; to alter, change; to turn aside, ward off, avert; to go over again, repeat, revise; to move (the hand) backwards and for wards (over), pass (the hand, &c. over, -par), to feel, to stroke, fondle, caress (the word hāth being expressed, e.g. us-par hāth phero); to pass (a brush, colour, &c. over, -par), to colour, whitewash, plaster (rang, &c. being expressed, e.g. us-par rang phero); to make (a horse, &c.) walk backwards and forwards; — to practise evasion, to double, shift, shuffle; to pervert, distort, or torture (another's words); to contradict (oneself)
subs: (intens. of phernā), v.t. To return, restore, refund; to send back, &c.
v.t. To take back; to withdraw; to reclaim; to walk (a horse, &c.) backwards and forwards; to bring (one round) or over (to one's side, kī t̤araf).

def: s.m. A jackal.
s.f. = pheṅṭ, q.v.
s.m. = pheṅṭā, q.v.
v.t. = pheṅṭnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Flattened ( = ćapṭā); flat-faced; — a shoe (of the kind called ćaṛhauwāṅ) with the heel turned down or flattened; a slipper.
s.f. The lash of a whip; the point of a scourge.
adj. (f. -ī), Sallow, pale, wan, faded, dim, dull, faint, light (of colour); tasteless, vapid, insipid (as food without salt, &c.); weak, watery, thin, poor, flat, jejune: dull, cheerless.

ety: Prk. पेक्खाइत्तो = S. प्रेक्ष्य˚+आपयन्; see pheṇknā
def: s.m. A thrower (of a spear, &c.); a skilled fencer, a club-player.
s.f. Playing with or using foils or clubs; the art of fencing.
v.t. To cause to throw; to teach to throw; to have or get (a thing) thrown away ( = phikwānā).
v.t. To bare or uncover the head; to unplait the hair, to let down the hair.
adj. = phīkā, q.v.
v.t. To cause (a thing) to be thrown or thrown away, to have (a thing) thrown away (by, se)
subs: v.t. To have (a thing) thrown away.

def: v.n. corr. of phisalnā, q.v.
s.f. The leg ( = pinḍlī, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to froth, to make frothy; to beat or whip into a froth ( = pheṅṭnā).
v.t. To squeeze; to wring out; to rinse, to wash
s.m. The fork-tailed shrike (probably a Prākr̤it form of the S. bhr̤ingaka).
adj. = phīkā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to be flung or thrown, &c. = phikānā; to cause to be chucked into the mouth; to chuck into the mouth ( = phāṅknā: in this sense the proper form is phaṅkānā).
v.n. To be flung or thrown; to be thrown away.
v.t. = phikwānā, q.v.
adv. Again; back, back again; afterwards, subsequently; thereafter, thereupon, then; in that case
subs: v.n. To come again, to come back, to return
adv. Again, yet, still, notwithstanding, even then; in spite of that
phir-phir-se, adv. Over and over again, repeatedly
v.t. To speak or say again, to say in reply, to retort

def: s.f. The act of returning; return; restitution, restoration.

v.t. To cause to be turned; to turn, to whirl, wheel; to turn round, wind, screw; to cause to go about, to move or to walk (a horse, &c.) up and down, or backwards and forwards; to turn aside or away; to return, give back; to turn back; to change; to roll, to shift
subs: v.t. To return, give back; to send back.

def: s.m. Return, restitution; turning, whirling, rotation.
s.m. Rejected things; return hire; expenses back (on a journey); — money paid by a lewd woman to her paramour.

ety: phirā, rt. of phirā-nā+ū = S. aff. उकः
def: adj. To be returned; taken conditionally, conditional or contingent (a purchase); — returning at stated periods, periodical; movable (as a feast-day, &c.).
adv. Over again, &c. = phir, q.v.
adv. = phir-kar; phir; q.v.
s.f. Whirligig, windmill (a child's toy); a reel (for thread, &c.); a spindle; anything that turns on an axis.

ety: phir˚ = pher˚ = Prk. फेरे(इ) or फेर(इ) = S. पर्ये(ति), rt. इ with परि
def: v.n. To turn, go round, revolve, whirl; to circulate; to turn back, to return; to walk, walk about, walk to and fro; to wander, rove, ramble, stroll; to travel; to turn over, to roll; to turn away, to turn (from, -se), to forsake (in this sense phir-jānā is the common form); to change; to turn aside, to deviate, wander; to turn, bend, become distorted or crooked, to warp; — to go forth, or to go (to the necessary), to have a stool (usually with jhāṛā or some similar word, e.g. jhāṛā phirnā)
subs: (-se), To turn away from (in dissatisfaction or displeasure), to be dissatisfied, be displeased
(-se), To pass (before); to go back, to return; to turn (from), turn (against), to forsake, abandon, desert, to revolt (from, or against); to be bent, be distorted; to warp.

def: s.m. Return, exchange.
